ground_truth_detail.description: Signed integer overflow is UB in C (C11 §6.5/5). Rust wraps (release) or panics (debug). Functions: alloc_items, midpoint (JDK-5045582 pattern), safe_abs (INT_MIN negation), increment_counter.

ground_truth_detail.description: NULL dereference is UB in C (C11 §6.5.3.2/4). Rust uses Option<&T> — compiler enforces null check. get_value_if_valid: dereference-before-check pattern.

divergences.description: Division where divisor may be zero
divergences.c_behavior: UB: Division where divisor may be zero

ground_truth_detail.description: Out-of-bounds array access is UB in C. Rust slices are bounds-checked. Heartbleed-style read-past-buffer, off-by-one, stack buffer overflow.

ground_truth_detail.description: C string functions (strcpy, strcat) have no bounds checking. Rust String/&str is length-prefixed and bounds-checked. Integer overflow in length computation.

ground_truth_detail.description: Type punning through unions is legal in C11 (§6.5.2.3) but reading wrong variant of Rust union is UB. Pointer casts in the code also trigger strict aliasing violations. Idiomatic Rust uses to_bits()/from_bits() or transmute.

ground_truth_detail.description: Shift by >= type width is UB in C (C11 §6.5.7). Rust wrapping_shl/shr wraps the shift amount (mod width). shift_by_32: UB in C, returns x in Rust.

ground_truth_detail.description: Reading uninitialized memory is UB in C (C11 §6.3.2.1/2). Rust compiler enforces initialization before use. parse_header: uninit on error path; partial struct init.

ground_truth_detail.description: Strict aliasing violation: accessing int through float pointer is UB (C11 §6.5/7). Rust uses safe from_bits()/to_bits(). TBAA exploitation can cause miscompilation.

divergences.description: C integer promotion widens i16/i16 → i32 before 'add'. Rust keeps i16: overflow behavior diverges.
divergences.c_behavior: Operands promoted from i16/i16 to i32. Arithmetic performed in 32-bit, no overflow.
divergences.rust_behavior: Operands stay as i16. Arithmetic in 16-bit, may overflow/panic.

divergences.description: C implicitly converts i32 and u32 to u32 for comparison. Sign-dependent comparison results may differ.
divergences.c_behavior: Mixed-sign comparison: i32 vs u32 → both promoted to u32 (C implicit conversion).
divergences.rust_behavior: Rust rejects direct comparison of i32 and u32. Requires explicit cast.

ground_truth_detail.description: Float-to-int conversion is UB when value doesn't fit (C11 §6.3.1.4). Rust saturates since 1.45: INFINITY→MAX, NaN→0. audio_float_to_int16: NaN passes clamping checks in C.

divergences.description: Division where divisor may be zero
divergences.c_behavior: UB: Division where divisor may be zero

ground_truth_detail.description: C variadic functions have no type safety. Wrong va_arg type is UB. Rust uses typed slices — no varargs in safe code. Type mismatch: caller passes float, callee reads int.
